root_text.an4.174:1.1: Atha kho āyasmā ānando yenāyasmā mahākoṭṭhiko tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmatā mahākoṭṭhikena saddhiṁ sammodi.
root_text.an4.174:1.2: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā ānando āyasmantaṁ mahākoṭṭhikaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an4.174:6.1: “Channaṁ, āvuso, phassāyatanānaṁ asesavirāganirodhā atthi ca natthi ca aññaṁ kiñcī”ti?
root_text.an4.174:8.1: “Channaṁ, āvuso, phassāyatanānaṁ asesavirāganirodhā nevatthi no natthaññaṁ kiñcī”ti?
root_text.an4.174:10.1: “‘Channaṁ, āvuso, phassāyatanānaṁ asesavirāganirodhā atthaññaṁ kiñcī’ti, iti puṭṭho samāno:
root_text.an4.174:10.3: ‘Channaṁ, āvuso, phassāyatanānaṁ asesavirāganirodhā natthaññaṁ kiñcī’ti, iti puṭṭho samāno:
root_text.an4.174:10.5: ‘Channaṁ, āvuso, phassāyatanānaṁ asesavirāganirodhā atthi ca natthi ca aññaṁ kiñcī’ti, iti puṭṭho samāno:
root_text.an4.174:10.7: ‘Channaṁ, āvuso, phassāyatanānaṁ asesavirāganirodhā nevatthi no natthaññaṁ kiñcī’ti, iti puṭṭho samāno:
root_text.an4.174:10.9: Yathā kathaṁ panāvuso, imassa bhāsitassa attho daṭṭhabbo”ti?
root_text.an4.174:11.1: “‘Channaṁ, āvuso, phassāyatanānaṁ asesavirāganirodhā atthaññaṁ kiñcī’ti, iti vadaṁ appapañcaṁ papañceti.
root_text.an4.174:11.2: ‘Channaṁ, āvuso, phassāyatanānaṁ asesavirāganirodhā natthaññaṁ kiñcī’ti, iti vadaṁ appapañcaṁ papañceti.
root_text.an4.174:11.3: ‘Channaṁ, āvuso, phassāyatanānaṁ asesavirāganirodhā atthi ca natthi ca aññaṁ kiñcī’ti, iti vadaṁ appapañcaṁ papañceti.
root_text.an4.174:11.4: ‘Channaṁ, āvuso, phassāyatanānaṁ asesavirāganirodhā nevatthi no natthaññaṁ kiñcī’ti, iti vadaṁ appapañcaṁ papañceti.
root_text.an4.174:11.5: Yāvatā, āvuso, channaṁ phassāyatanānaṁ gati tāvatā papañcassa gati.
root_text.an4.174:11.6: Yāvatā papañcassa gati tāvatā channaṁ phassāyatanānaṁ gati.
root_text.an4.174:11.7: Channaṁ, āvuso, phassāyatanānaṁ asesavirāganirodhā papañcanirodho papañcavūpasamo”ti.

translation_text.an4.174:1.1: Then Venerable Ānanda went up to Venerable Mahākoṭṭhita, and exchanged greetings with him.
translation_text.an4.174:1.2: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, Ānanda sat down to one side, and said to Mahākoṭṭhita:
translation_text.an4.174:2.1: “Reverend, when these six fields of contact have faded away and ceased with nothing left over, does something else exist?”
translation_text.an4.174:6.1: “Does something else both still exist and no longer exist?”
translation_text.an4.174:8.1: “Does something else neither still exist nor no longer exist?”
translation_text.an4.174:10.2: you say ‘don’t put it like that’.
translation_text.an4.174:10.9: … How then should we see the meaning of this statement?”
translation_text.an4.174:11.1: “In saying that, ‘when the six fields of contact have faded away and ceased with nothing left over, something else exists’, you’re proliferating the unproliferated.
translation_text.an4.174:11.2: In saying that, ‘something else no longer exists’, you’re proliferating the unproliferated.
translation_text.an4.174:11.3: In saying that, ‘something else both still exists and no longer exists’, you’re proliferating the unproliferated.
translation_text.an4.174:11.4: In saying that, ‘something else neither still exists nor no longer exists’, you’re proliferating the unproliferated.
translation_text.an4.174:11.5: The scope of proliferation extends as far as the scope of the six fields of contact.
translation_text.an4.174:11.6: The scope of the six fields of contact extends as far as the scope of proliferation.
translation_text.an4.174:11.7: When the six fields of contact fade away and cease with nothing left over, proliferation stops and is stilled.”
